planning_gate.spec_delta: M19 tests a contact-constrained qfrc action wrapper that optimizes stance preload and knee/hip pose assist together.

perspectives.product: tests whether the same G1 can keep foot contact while adding the missing visible knee flexion
perspectives.architecture: keeps exp87's 3-phase teacher and adds contact-gated knee/hip qfrc pose assist plus stance preload before stepping MuJoCo
perspectives.security: no credentials or external side effects
perspectives.qa: native sweep records raw JSON, visible gate metrics, pose-qfrc candidates, and optimizer score per variant
perspectives.skeptic: direct knee torque can improve pose metrics while destabilizing support, slip, or return

dod: raw native JSON per pose-qfrc variant
summary ranks variants and states whether any variant passes visible_8cm_gate

sources.note: IEEE Robots Guide shows Unitree G1 in a deep squat-like pose, supporting kinematic plausibility before dynamics testing.
Humanoid squat research frames squatting as a whole-body coordination task requiring foot-force and dynamic constraints.
Whole-body constrained learning motivates adding contact and foot-terrain constraints in a low-level whole-body follower instead of reward-only tuning.
MuJoCo whole-body MPC work supports using simulator dynamics and contact modeling for legged whole-body control experiments.
